title: Jaitak Fort
tagline: Jaitak Fort — heritage city of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Jaitak Fort is situated about 15 km from district headquarters Nahan in Sirmaur district in the state of Himachal Pradesh in India.
overview.description: Jaitak Fort is situated about 15 km from district headquarters Nahan in Sirmaur district in the state of Himachal Pradesh in India. Situated 1,479 meters above sea level, Jaitak Fort is notable due to its location and its view of the surrounding countryside due to its strategic setting.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Jaithak
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Jaithak is the subsequent battle fought by the 53rd division of East India Company after the Battle of Nalapani against Nepalese forces. Nalapani had cost both sides dearly, but in Nahan and Jaithak further west, they were to suffer more.

topPlaces.name: Nahan
topPlaces.description: Nahan is a town located in the Sirmaur district of the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It serves as the administrative headquarters of Sirmaur district.

topPlaces.name: Roranwali
topPlaces.description: Roranwali is a village in Hanumangarh district, Rajasthan state of western India. The town of Hanumangarh is the district headquarters.

items.note: The Battle of Jaithak is the subsequent battle fought by the 53rd division of East India Company after the Battle of Nalapani against Nepalese forces. Nalapani had cost both sides dearly, but in Nahan and Jaithak further west, they were to suffer more.
Nahan is a town located in the Sirmaur district of the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It serves as the administrative headquarters of Sirmaur district.
Roranwali is a village in Hanumangarh district, Rajasthan state of western India. The town of Hanumangarh is the district headquarters.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jaitak Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jaitak Fort is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jaitak Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (53 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (50 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jaitak Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jaitak Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jaitak Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Jaitak Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jaitak Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Battle of Jaithak, Nahan, Roranwali.
